mnemonics: CAR PRICE: Categorical And Regression Predictors Inflate Car Expenses
keyPoints: The goal is to predict a continuous numerical value (price) using a regression model.
Features can include numerical (mileage, age) and categorical data (brand, fuel type), requiring proper preprocessing like one-hot encoding.
Model evaluation relies on metrics like RMSE (Root Mean Squed Error) and MAE (Mean Absolute Error) to measure prediction accuracy.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to scale or normalize numerical features, which can bias models sensitive to feature magnitude like SVMs or gradient descent.
Improperly handling categorical variables by label encoding them as arbitrary integers, which implies an order that doesn't exist (e.g., assigning Honda=1, BMW=2, Toyota=3).
Overfitting the model to the training data by not using techniques like regularization or cross-validation, leading to poor performance on new, unseen car data.
